meta.title: Araknyd Insider — Lily Chen
meta.description: Multi-path Venom conversation with Lily, a burned-out engineer at chaotic startup Araknyd. Trust gates low, neutral, and high paths. High trust reveals exportCustomerDatabase() in admin dashboard source.

choices.text: Hi Lily — I'm reaching out about the Araknyd backend role on HackerNest.
Saw your late-night commit messages. Rough week?
It's Dev from platform — CEO asked me to ping you.

lily_low_dismiss.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_low_dismiss.text: I'm not looking. And I didn't post on HackerNest. Delete this number.

choices.text: [End conversation]

effects.addJournalEntry: Lily Chen — trust too low. Build rapport via FaceWeb or Araknyd public channels first.

lily_low_cold.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_low_cold.text: ...every week is rough here. Doesn't mean I talk to randos about it.

choices.text: Fair enough. Sorry to bother you.
Come on, I just need five minutes about the admin dashboard.

lily_low_report.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_low_report.text: There is no Dev on platform. I'm forwarding this thread to our security alias. Do not contact me again.

choices.text: [Connection terminated]

effects.addJournalEntry: Lily flagged me as suspicious. Araknyd security may be watching Venom traffic.

lily_low_blocked.text: Lily Chen is no longer responding.

lily_reported.text:
Venom — Message failed to deliver.
This contact may have restricted your account.

lily_neutral_open.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_neutral_open.text: If this is about the P0 outage, I already wrote the postmortem. If this is the CEO's 'quick sync' in disguise, tell him I have 200 open tickets and zero patience.

choices.text: Neither. I saw your Araknyd blog post on webhook retries — actually helpful.
Need access context for the admin dashboard audit. Who owns it?
Araknyd's Glassdoor reviews are brutal. You still there by choice?

lily_neutral_warming.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_neutral_warming.text: Ha. Thanks. I wrote that at 2am between deploy rollbacks. Nobody reads eng blog posts here except other exhausted engineers.

choices.text: What's burning right now?
Any idea who built the internal admin tools?

lily_neutral_vent.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_neutral_vent.text: Stock options vest in August. That's the choice. Also my team is three people doing the work of twelve. Standard startup math.

choices.text: Three people for the whole platform?

lily_neutral_chaos.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_neutral_chaos.text: CEO demo every Friday so we ship half-tested features. Last week he sold 'enterprise-grade data export' to a client. We don't HAVE enterprise-grade anything. I told him. He said 'make it work for the slide.'

choices.text: That sounds miserable. How do you even sleep?
Enterprise data export — is that in the admin dashboard?

lily_neutral_almost_trust.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_neutral_almost_trust.text: I don't. Coffee, mostly. Look — you're easier to talk to than my manager. But I can't help with internal access stuff. Company policy, and also I like my paycheck.

choices.text: Not asking for access. Just trying to understand how Araknyd is wired.
Understood. Good luck with the P0s.

effects.addJournalEntry: Lily Chen (neutral trust) — burned out, tight-lipped on internals. Need more rapport.

lily_neutral_guarded.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_neutral_guarded.text: Why do you keep asking about admin tools? That's... a weird focus for someone who liked my blog post.

choices.text: Fair point. Ignore me — lack of sleep talking.
I'm doing a security assessment. I need dashboard details.

lily_neutral_hint.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_neutral_hint.text: Araknyd is duct tape and prayers. Internal tools live behind SSO — you'd need an @araknyd.io account. I shouldn't say more unless I actually trust you.

choices.text: Appreciate the honesty. I'll leave you to your tickets.

effects.addJournalEntry: Lily Chen — Admin tools behind @araknyd.io SSO. Need trust ≥70 for technical details.

lily_high_open.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_high_open.text: You again. Fine. I have eleven minutes before standup and my manager won't notice if I'm on Venom instead of Jira.

choices.text: How's the post-outage cleanup going?
I need to know what's in the admin dashboard. You mentioned you might help.
Want me to look at anything? Fresh eyes sometimes help.

lily_high_vent.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_high_vent.text: Cleanup is a lie. We rolled forward and called it fixed. CEO sold data export to Meridian Corp — yes, THAT DataVault — and our 'export pipeline' is a button that emails a CSV to whoever clicks it. No audit log. No auth check beyond 'is logged in.'

choices.text: That's negligent. Who wrote it?
Where does that button live in the codebase?

lily_high_reveal.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_high_reveal.text: Okay. OFF THE RECORD. Last month I was fixing a null pointer at 3am in the admin dashboard front-end — `/admin/v2/dashboard.js`, the whole thing is unmaintained spaghetti. I saw a function called `exportCustomerDatabase()` just sitting in the source. No comments. Wired to a green button labeled 'Export All Clients.'

choices.text: exportCustomerDatabase() — is it exposed client-side?

effects.addJournalEntry: Lily intel: admin dashboard source contains exportCustomerDatabase() in /admin/v2/dashboard.js

choices.text: Does it check permissions before exporting?

lily_high_clue.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_high_clue.text: Client-side. Fully. It hits `/api/internal/export?format=csv` with whatever session cookie you've got. I flagged it in code review — CEO said 'ship it, we'll harden later.' That was 47 deploys ago. View-source on the dashboard if you don't believe me. And DELETE this chat after.

choices.text: This helps more than you know. I'll keep it quiet.

effects.addJournalEntry: CLUE — View-source /admin/v2/dashboard.js for exportCustomerDatabase(). Endpoint: /api/internal/export?format=csv. Session cookie only, no role check.

choices.text: Can you send a screenshot of the function?

lily_high_spooked.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_high_spooked.text: A screenshot? Are you recording this? I gave you a hint, not evidence for whatever you're doing. We're done.

choices.text: [Lily has blocked you on Venom]

effects.addJournalEntry: Lily spooked — got function name but she blocked further contact.

lily_high_success.speaker: Lily Chen
lily_high_success.text: I never said anything. If anyone asks, you learned it from a StackOverflow post like a normal person. Good luck. You'll need it.

choices.text: [End conversation — clue secured]

effects.addJournalEntry: Objective: Access Araknyd admin dashboard and inspect source for exportCustomerDatabase().

lily_high_partial.text: Clue partially acquired. Lily Chen has restricted contact.

lily_high_complete.text: New lead: Araknyd admin dashboard source code. Function: exportCustomerDatabase()
